items.title: Derivatives
items.description: The chain rule combines elementary derivatives (power, exp, trig) from the derivative rules you already know.

items.title: Partial derivatives
items.description: When inputs are multivariate, the chain rule uses partial derivatives and sums paths—essential for layers with many neurons.

items.title: Gradients
items.description: Vector-valued compositions yield gradients via the chain rule; gradient descent consumes the result of chained local derivatives.

items.title: Jacobian and Hessian
items.description: Jacobians package all first-order chain-rule partials for vector functions; Hessians arise from chaining twice for curvature.

items.title: Layers and neurons
items.description: Each layer is a composed transformation; stacking layers multiplies chain-rule factors during backpropagation.

items.title: Chain rule in networks
items.description: Dedicated treatment of how the chain rule scales to deep architectures, skip connections and fused ops.
